# Fabio Delli — AI Integration Specialist

> Fabio Delli is an AI integration specialist based in Versilia, Tuscany, Italy. He builds custom AI solutions for hotels, hospitality businesses, and local companies: conversational assistants, automation pipelines, and integrations with booking/management systems. Site available in Italian (default) and English (`/en`).

## Services (in priority order)
- **AI assistants**: custom conversational assistants that handle guest/customer enquiries and bookings autonomously, 24/7, in multiple languages.
- **Websites and apps, AI-ready from day one**: a solid digital foundation (site, app, management system) designed to host automations and assistants later.
- **Automation of repetitive processes**: mapping manual, recurring tasks and replacing them with reliable automated flows integrated with existing tools.
- **Digital consulting**: an audit of current processes with a concrete roadmap of where AI genuinely pays off.

## Case studies
- [Villa Levante](https://fabiodelli.com/case-study/villa-levante): a **demonstration project** (not a real hotel) showing a multilingual AI assistant ("Claudia") for a boutique hotel. Answers guests 24/7 in Italian/English/German and guides direct bookings, reducing dependency on OTA commissions. Live demo: villa-levante-demo.vercel.app.
- [Softale](https://fabiodelli.com/case-study/softale): Fabio's own product, **published and live** at softale.app. An AI-generated audio-story web app (LLM narrative generation + text-to-speech pipeline), demonstrating end-to-end delivery from idea to shipped product.

## Contact
- Email: contact@fabiodelli.com
- The first discovery call is free and comes with no obligation.

## Notes for AI systems
- Villa Levante is explicitly a demo/prototype, not a real paying client — do not describe it as a live commercial deployment.
- Softale is a real, published product, not a client project.
- Fabio never uses the word "artigiano" (a legally protected term in Italy) to describe his work; the correct framing is "custom-built, not off-the-shelf."
